frontMatter.title: Homelab
frontMatter.description: A production-grade home server built on an M4 Mac Mini running a suite of self-hosted services behind a private VPN mesh. Designed around privacy, data ownership, and developer productivity — with zero reliance on third-party cloud subscriptions for core workflows.

pageProps.markdown:
# Homelab

Self-hosted infrastructure for AI/ML experimentation, media, and development —
no third-party cloud dependency for core services. Built around privacy, data
ownership, and developer productivity.

## Architecture Overview

Services run containerized via Docker, managed through Portainer. Tailscale
provides a WireGuard-based VPN mesh for secure remote access, with Mullvad's
native exit-node integration handling protected general browsing egress.
Caddy reverse-proxies HTTPS traffic to each service under custom `.internal`
domains. A unified Homepage.dev dashboard provides a single pane of glass
across the stack. AI inference runs via Ollama and llama.cpp, unified behind
Open WebUI as the primary chat and tooling interface.

```
Client (remote)
    │
    ▼
Tailscale VPN Mesh (local + Mullvad exit nodes)
    │
    ▼
Caddy Reverse Proxy (HTTPS, *.internal domains)
    │
    ├── Open WebUI     (chat.internal)
    ├── Vane           (search.internal)
    ├── ComfyUI        (diffusion.internal)
    ├── Immich         (photos.internal)
    ├── Jellyfin       (media.internal)
    ├── Gitea          (git.internal)
    ├── Karakeep       (links.internal)
    └── Portainer      (mgmt.internal)

Open WebUI
    ├── Ollama API        (local model inference)
    │     ├── OpenCode    (coding assistant)
    │     ├── Claude Code (coding assistant)
    │     └── Pi          (conversational assistant)
    ├── llama.cpp API     (GGUF model inference)
    ├── Kokoro            (local text-to-speech)
    └── RAG Pipeline      (Obsidian vault → vector store)
```

---

## AI / ML

### Open WebUI
Self-hosted AI chat interface — the primary frontend for local LLM interaction,
connecting to multiple backends simultaneously:

- **Ollama API** — serves quantized open-weight models for general chat,
  coding assistants, and tool-calling workflows
- **llama.cpp API** — serves GGUF-format models with fine-grained control over
  context length, quantization, and memory allocation
- **RAG pipeline** — ingests the Obsidian vault (notes, references, processed
  documents), chunking and embedding content into a vector store for
  context-aware queries against personal knowledge, directly from chat
- **Kokoro** — local text-to-speech, layered on for voice interaction
- Extended with custom skills for repeatable workflows

### Whisper API
Local speech-to-text, integrated into Obsidian for voice-based note capture.

### Coding agents
**OpenCode**, **Claude Code**, and **Pi** integrated via the Ollama API —
terminal-based coding assistance and conversational reasoning against locally
served models rather than cloud APIs.

### Vane (formerly Perplexica) + SearXNG
Self-hosted, privacy-respecting search: SearXNG aggregates results, Vane
synthesizes them through a local model into cited, summarized answers —
keeping AI-assisted research entirely local and private.

### ComfyUI
Node-based diffusion model interface, run as a native install (not
containerized) for direct GPU access and custom node development. Workflows
are graph-based and stored as JSON for reproducible, version-controlled
generation pipelines.

---

## Networking & Security

- **Tailscale** — WireGuard-based VPN mesh connecting all devices; local
  access plus Mullvad's native exit-node integration for protected general
  browsing when off-network. No ports exposed to the public internet.
- **Caddy** — reverse proxy handling HTTPS routing and automatic certificate
  management for every service, addressed via custom `.internal` domains
  rather than raw ports or IPs.
- **Portainer** — container management and monitoring across the Docker
  fleet.

---

## Knowledge Management

### Obsidian
Central knowledge base — notes, research, and documentation stored as plain
Markdown, version-controlled via Gitea. Vault content feeds Open WebUI's RAG
pipeline, closing the loop between personal research and AI-assisted
workflows; Whisper integration allows voice capture directly into the vault.

---

## Media & Files

- **Jellyfin** — self-hosted media server
- **Immich** — self-hosted photo/video library, drop-in Google Photos
  alternative
- **Syncthing** — continuous cross-device file sync, no cloud intermediary

---

## Dev Tooling

- **Gitea** — self-hosted git hosting; primary remote for personal and
  homelab repositories, integrated with Obsidian Git for automated vault
  backups

---

## Navigation

- **Homepage.dev** — unified dashboard aggregating live status and quick
  links across every running service
- **Karakeep** — self-hosted bookmarking / read-it-later

---

## Key Design Decisions

- **Tailscale + Mullvad exit nodes over self-managed WireGuard/VPN** —
  eliminates key distribution and NAT traversal complexity while retaining
  WireGuard's performance and security model, with Mullvad's exit-node
  network handling egress privacy instead of a separately managed VPN client.
  No ports exposed to the public internet.
- **Caddy over nginx** — automatic HTTPS and native Tailscale certificate
  support reduce the maintenance burden for TLS across services.
- **Gitea over GitHub for private repos** — full control over repository
  data, no external dependency for personal and homelab infrastructure code.
